urgency_rules.description: Urgency level definitions, criteria, response targets, and trigger phrase lists.

levels.description: Immediate escalation required. Do not leave in standard queue.
criteria: Regulatory or government body mentioned (ICO, FTC, ombudsman, watchdog)
Data breach suspicion reported by customer
Service completely down for a paying customer on a critical plan

levels.description: Senior agent required. Expedite but draft response can be prepared.
criteria: Repeat contact: 3 or more times for the same issue
Significant financial impact (threshold configurable — default >$500 or business-critical)
VIP or high-value account with negative experience
Public-facing damage possible but not yet critical
levels.queue_position: PRIORITY — ahead of MEDIUM and LOW

levels.description: Standard handling. Clear resolution path exists. No unusual urgency.
criteria: First contact for a common, resolvable issue
No deadline or SLA concern stated
Billing, order, or technical issue with a standard fix

levels.description: Non-urgent. Handle in order. No time pressure.
criteria: Spam or off-topic message requiring only a brief response or no response

trigger_phrase_overrides.description: These phrases auto-trigger CRITICAL urgency regardless of other signals.
trigger_phrase_overrides.action: Set urgency to CRITICAL and flag for immediate human review. Do not rely on draft response alone.

override_policy.description: Human agents may override AI-assigned urgency levels.
override_policy.note: Track override rate weekly. Target <15% override rate. High override rate signals recalibration needed.
